content:
💡 DroidDesk | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: Transforming an Android phone into a portable Linux PC capable of running a wide range of desktop applications.
✨ Key Features: • Run full Linux desktop on Android • Direct kernel access, no emulation • Support for XFCE4, LXQt, MATE, KDE desktop environments
📖 Summary: DroidDesk enables users to run a full Linux desktop environment on their Android phone, leveraging Termux, Termux X11, and Proot. It allows for the installation and execution of various Linux applications, including development tools, office suites, and even demanding software like Blender, with support for both on-device display and external monitor output via USB-C or a Raspberry Pi bridge.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
